source_pr.title: executor: pessimistic txn update forUpdateTS for concurrent insert
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
A SELECT FOR UPDATE statement in a pessimistic transaction may not see already committed inserted rows.

### What is changed and how it works?
Get a new forUpdateTS if the `SELECT FOR UPDATE|UPDATE|DELETE` statement is not PointGet plan.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change
 - Has exported variable/fields change
 - Has interface methods change
 - Has persistent data change

Side effects

 - Possible performance regression

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

Release note

 - fix the issue that SELECT FOR UPDATE statement may not see already committed concurrent insert.

symptoms: Go race detector reports a DATA RACE when tests mutate TiKV global knobs (e.g. `PrewriteMaxBackoff`) while other goroutines/tests may read them.
failure_signature: DATA RACE detected under `-race`, involving concurrent access to global TiKV test knobs (e.g. `tikv.PrewriteMaxBackoff`) mutated in `session/pessimistic_test.go`.
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is due to a real data race on shared global state: the tests change global TiKV settings (e.g. `tikv.PrewriteMaxBackoff`) and if tests/goroutines run concurrently this can trigger `-race` failures or cross-test interference. The PR stabilizes it by moving the global assignment into suite setup and restoring it in suite teardown (with proper ordering), so the mutation is controlled and cleaned up reliably.
fix_pattern: Avoid data races from global test knobs: set global variables in suite-level setup (run once) and restore them in teardown, rather than mutating shared globals during concurrent test execution.
